title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss joined the Patriots from the Raiders in 2007, setting an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in one season.
Wes Welker, acquired from the Dolphins in 2007, became a prolific slot receiver for the Patriots alongside Randy Moss.
Josh Gordon joined the Patriots in 2018, recording over 700 yards in 11 games but struggled with off-field issues.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected for arguing a strike three call during a game against the Cincinnati Reds.
Cora disagreed with umpire C.B. Bucknor's decision not to appeal Trevor Story's check swing to the first base umpire.
Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony criticized himself for a failed challenge that left the team without further ABS challenges.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story objected to umpire C.B. Bucknor's strike three call without consulting the first base umpire, leading to Alex Cora's ejection.
Despite Story's early home run, the Red Sox lost 6-5 to the Twins, struggling offensively except for two key homers.
The Red Sox are now 1-1 this season after failing to complete a comeback, losing in the 11th inning to the Twins.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha scores 11 goals in 16 games since Bruins' return from Olympic break, missing games due to concussion.
Zacha's performance drives Bruins' second line, contributing to team's success with 26 goals this season, career-high for him.
Traded to Bruins four years ago, Zacha evolves from underwhelming asset to key player, nearing career-high 60 points.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray allowed four runs over four innings in his Red Sox debut against the Cincinnati Reds, ending in a 6-5 loss.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora said Gray settled in despite early struggles, highlighting the defense's role in the rough start.
The Red Sox bullpen, including Danny Coulombe and Aroldis Chapman, kept the team in the game until Justin Slaten allowed a walk-off hit.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands gathered at Boston Common for the third nationwide 'No Kings' protest, advocating for democracy over authoritarianism.
Governor Maura Healey and Senator Elizabeth Warren were among the speakers addressing political and social issues, including immigration and military policy.
The protest was part of over 3,300 rallies nationwide, with more than 160 held in Massachusetts, emphasizing civic engagement and advocacy.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute set with 17 songs at Boston's House of Blues, thrilling fans with intense theatrics.
Lead singer Michael Bishop led the band in spraying fake blood at the audience, maintaining their shock rock tradition.
The setlist included a cover of 'Pink Pony Club' by Chappell Roan and ended with 'Sick of You' as a tribute.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Four Celtics players, including Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum, are questionable for Sunday's game against the Hornets.
Jayson Tatum struggles with shooting but contributed 26 points and 12 rebounds in Friday's win over Atlanta.
Nikola Vucevic remains out with a right ring finger fracture as Celtics assess injury statuses on game day.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands attended the 'No Kings' protest in Boston Common, featuring speeches by Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren.
Veterans and church groups participated, raising concerns over democracy's future and issues like increasing global tensions.
Protesters wore costumes symbolizing opposition to monarchy while others, like Susan Tully, chose to participate on their birthday.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered at Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest on March 28, part of a nationwide demonstration.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the crowd during the Boston protest.
Over 160 'No Kings' events occurred across Massachusetts, with significant participation in Boston's Public Garden and Common.
